fields.description:
## Why
The shared `impact = mean(severity attributes)` dilutes a genuinely high-severity plan finding below the
0.70 bar (a critical finding averaged with low axes lands ~0.60-0.69). The mean is the wrong aggregator.

## What
For the plan-review gate, replace the mean with severity-first MAX over the axis contributions, a hard
override, and a detection amplifier, dispatched per-gate off `decide.py`. Concrete definitions:
- SEVERITY axes (each mapped to a contribution in [0,1] by the verify pass): AC-unverifiable,
  DoD-uncertifiable, undecomposed (from the deterministic G5 signal), divergent-implementation,
  internal-conflict, vague-directive, irreversible-without-rationale.
- AGGREGATE: `impact_sev = MAX(axis contributions)` (no averaging).
- HARD OVERRIDE: if ANY of {AC-unverifiable, DoD-uncertifiable, undecomposed, divergent-implementation}
  is true, `impact_sev = max(impact_sev, 0.85)` regardless of other axes (FMEA Action-Priority pattern).
- DETECTION AMPLIFIER (a multiplier, defined here): `silent-vs-self-revealing` = does building to the
  flawed plan fail silently (agent confidently builds the wrong thing) vs self-revealingly (agent hits a
  wall)? silent -> x1.0, self-revealing -> x0.8. `DoD-uncertifiable` also forces the low-detectability
  (x1.0) path. `impact = min(1.0, impact_sev * detection_mult)`.
- PER-GATE DISPATCH: `decide.py` selects `impact_plan()` vs the code-review impact by an injected gate
  identity, exactly as `threshold_for(gate=...)` already dispatches; the plan-review signed-verdict shape
  stays byte-compatible.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` (per-gate `impact()`), the plan-review verify prompt/schema
  (emit the axis sub-answers listed above), and the per-gate dispatch wiring in the orchestrator.
- Depends on the deterministic G5 signal child (for the `undecomposed` input). No code-review changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `decide.py` computes plan-review `impact = MAX(axes)` with the hard-override list above, replacing
      the mean for this gate ONLY; the code-review path is byte-unchanged by this child.
- [ ] The detection amplifier is implemented with the stated multipliers (silent x1.0 / self-revealing x0.8;
      DoD-uncertifiable forces x1.0).
- [ ] Per-gate `impact()` dispatch mirrors `threshold_for`; a test asserts the plan verdict shape is
      byte-compatible with the prior signed shape.
- [ ] Offline replay: previously-stranded 0.60-0.69 band findings clear the 0.70 bar; the recorded verdict
      set stays stable on same-material replicate pairs.
